goal: Generate a formal instrumented-hardware PFL validation test protocol for 4 sim-identified worst-case contact configurations, with pass/fail criteria tied to ISO/TS 15066 Table A.2.
thoughts: Never quote ISO/TS 15066 Table A.2 rows from memory. export_scene_package archives each sim scenario as a traceable input artifact (sim narrows the search; it does NOT close the case). lookup_knowledge pulls the actual biomechanical table. lookup_product_spec retrieves FUTEK / Pressurex / Kistler calibration requirements. Minimum n=3 trials per condition — refuse n=1. Never blend simulated and measured values.
code:
# Sim narrows the search. Hardware test closes the case.

# 1. Archive each of the 4 candidate scenarios as versioned test inputs
for scenario_id in ["<WC_1>", "<WC_2>", "<WC_3>", "<WC_4>"]:
    export_scene_package(
        stage_path=f"<CANDIDATES_DIR>/{scenario_id}.usd",
        output_path=f"<TEST_PROTOCOL_INPUTS>/{scenario_id}.zip",
        include_referenced_assets=True,
        pin_versions=True
    )

# 2. Pull the biomechanical table (transient + quasi-static per body region)
lookup_knowledge(query="ISO TS 15066 Annex A Table A.2 body region transient quasi-static force limits")

# 3. Pull instrument calibration specs
lookup_product_spec(product="FUTEK LSB200", field="iso_17025_calibration_cycle")
lookup_product_spec(product="Pressurex Super Low", field="range_response_time")
lookup_product_spec(product="Kistler 9317C", field="biofidelic_pad_spec")

# Protocol structure (template — fill each <...> from retrieved data, not memory):
#   1. Objective: validate PFL at <N> sim-identified worst-case locations
#   2. Traceable inputs: scenario archive IDs <WC_1>..<WC_4> (pinned)
#   3. Equipment + ISO 17025 calibration evidence: <FUTEK>, <PRESSUREX>, <KISTLER>
#   4. Environmental conditions: <TEMP_RANGE>, <HUMIDITY>
#   5. Per-location plan:
#        body region <X> (ISO/TS 15066 row)
#        transient limit: <FROM_ISO>
#        quasi-static limit: <FROM_ISO>
#        min trials: 3 per robot speed (n>=3, no exceptions)
#        recorded fields: peak_force_N, contact_duration_ms, effective_mass_kg
#   6. Acceptance: mean + 1 stddev must remain below ISO limit
#   7. Sign-off: test engineer / safety engineer / witness
# STATE EXPLICITLY: passing the physical test is what closes the safety case.
# Sim values never appear in the 'measured' column.
failure_modes: Accepting n=1 trial per condition (not statistically defensible)
Blending simulated and measured force values in a single 'result' column
Using the sim-reported peak force as a measured value
Citing ISO/TS 15066 table rows from memory instead of lookup_knowledge
